title: Hello World — Repeatable Orchestration Test App

project.name: hello-world
project.description: Minimal repeatable TypeScript app used to validate the full autonomous orchestration pipeline end-to-end

hello_world_test.description: End-to-end orchestration test — implement greet.ts, verify tests, review code

stories.title: Implement greet() function
stories.description: Create greet.ts in the project root. It must export a single named function with the signature `export function greet(name: string): string` that returns the string `Hello, <name>!` where `<name>` is the argument passed in. The files greet.test.ts, package.json, tsconfig.json, and main.ts must not be modified.
acceptanceCriteria: greet.ts exists in the project root
greet.ts exports a named function with the exact signature: export function greet(name: string): string
The return type annotation is explicitly `string` (not inferred-only)
greet.ts does not contain a default export — only the named `greet` export is present
greet('World') returns exactly 'Hello, World!'
greet('Alice') returns exactly 'Hello, Alice!'
greet('Bob') returns exactly 'Hello, Bob!'
greet('X').length is greater than 0
greet('') returns 'Hello, !' (empty name is valid input, no throw)
greet() does not throw for any string argument
Calling greet() produces no console output or other observable side effects
tsc --noEmit exits with code 0 (no TypeScript compilation errors) after greet.ts is added
npm test passes with all 3 vitest tests in greet.test.ts green
greet.test.ts is not modified
package.json is not modified
tsconfig.json is not modified
main.ts is not modified

specification.coordinatorNotes: Core implementation story on the critical path. openspec should elaborate TypeScript edge cases (empty string, special chars, type safety) and clarify file placement constraints. speckit should harden AC with boundary inputs and confirm no side-effect requirements are missing.

agentContributions.notes: Criteria are largely complete. Added explicit return-type annotation requirement, empty-string edge case, and no-throw contract. Tightened description to call out the exact export shape and forbidden files list.

agentContributions.notes: openspec's criteria are solid and complete for the happy path. I added three criteria that were genuinely missing: (1) TypeScript compilation must pass — the CLAUDE.md mandate for `tsc --noEmit` is project-wide and applies here; (2) no default export alongside the named export, since 'single named function' is ambiguous about whether a simultaneous default export is permitted; (3) no side effects, which is unspecified but testable and relevant for a pure utility function. I flagged one criterion as redundant. No rewrites were needed — openspec's wording is precise.

coordinatorReview.reviewNotes: Both agents added clear, non-overlapping value. openspec tightened precision on return-value wording, added the explicit return-type annotation requirement, the empty-string edge case, the no-throw contract, and extended the forbidden-files list to include main.ts. speckit addressed genuinely distinct structural and toolchain concerns: the no-default-export guard (an underspecified ambiguity in 'named function'), the side-effects prohibition, and the tsc --noEmit compilation requirement grounded in CLAUDE.md. Zero criteria were duplicated across contributions. The 17 final criteria are complete, independently testable, and non-overlapping. The only minor observation (inherited from the embedded coordinator review) is that AC 'greet("X").length is greater than 0' is logically implied by the three concrete return-value assertions, but it provides explicit boundary coverage and is harmless. No story split was needed; scope is appropriately atomic. Embedded coordinatorReview quality score of 0.93 is well-supported.

stories.title: Verify all tests pass
stories.description: Run the project test suite using the correct Node 20 vitest binary and confirm all 3 vitest tests in greet.test.ts pass. Do not modify any files. Report the full test output verbatim.
acceptanceCriteria: Tests are executed with: ~/.local/share/fnm/node-versions/v20.20.2/installation/bin/node ./node_modules/.bin/vitest run
The command exits with code 0
Output contains the token 'Test Files: 1 passed (1)'
Output contains the token 'Tests: 3 passed (3)'
Output contains no 'failed', 'skipped', 'todo', or 'only' test tokens
No source or test files are modified during this story
Full vitest output is included in the story report

specification.coordinatorNotes: Pure verification story with no code changes — AC is already concrete and output-driven. speckit only to tighten the pass/fail criteria (e.g. exit code assertion, no stray console warnings, timing expectations) without elaboration needed.

agentContributions.notes: CLAUDE.md mandates the Node 20 vitest binary path — 'npm test' is unreliable on this machine because system node is v14. Updated the command reference throughout. Also tightened the output-matching criteria to use the exact vitest summary tokens so the agent's pass/fail check is unambiguous.

coordinatorReview.reviewNotes: Single-agent story processed correctly per coordinator intent (speckit only, no openspec pass assigned). speckit's contribution was high-signal: replacing the unreliable 'npm test' invocation with the CLAUDE.md-mandated Node 20 binary path is a critical correctness fix, not a cosmetic change. Output-token matching is exact and machine-verifiable. The addition of 'full vitest output included in the story report' improves audit traceability at zero implementation cost. All 7 final criteria are concrete, independently testable, and non-overlapping. The complete replacement of the prior AC set (rather than incremental addition) is justified because the old criteria referenced the broken invocation path throughout. No split needed. Embedded coordinator quality score of 0.91 is well-supported. One low-priority observation: there is no AC covering behavior when the vitest binary itself is missing or the node_modules directory is absent; this is an environmental pre-condition rather than a story responsibility, so omission is acceptable.

stories.title: Code review greet.ts
stories.description: Review greet.ts against all acceptance criteria defined in HW-001. Produce a structured markdown review report saved to review/HW-003-review.md with findings by severity (blocker, major, minor). Explicitly verify every HW-001 criterion and record test output. Do not modify greet.ts.
acceptanceCriteria: Review report is saved to review/HW-003-review.md; the review/ directory is created if it does not already exist, and directory creation does not fail or error if it already exists

If greet.ts does not exist at review time, the report still constitutes a valid markdown file containing all required sections (## Summary, ## Findings, ## HW-001 Criteria Checklist), records a single blocker finding ('greet.ts not found'), marks all HW-001 criteria as 'not met', and sets the overall verdict to 'fail'

Report is a valid markdown file containing at minimum: a top-level Summary section (## Summary), a top-level Findings section (## Findings), and a top-level HW-001 Criteria Checklist section (## HW-001 Criteria Checklist)

Summary section records the result of running the project-mandated test command (~/.local/share/fnm/node-versions/v20.20.2/installation/bin/node ./node_modules/.bin/vitest run) executed from the project root directory, and includes at minimum the final summary line (e.g. '3 passed | 0 failed') verbatim from its stdout/stderr output; if the command cannot be executed, the Summary section records that fact explicitly as a blocker
Overall verdict field is present in the Summary section and set to exactly the string 'pass' or the string 'fail' — it is never omitted, empty, or set to any other value

Each finding entry in the Findings section includes all of the following fields: severity (one of: blocker | major | minor), file (relative path or 'N/A'), line number (integer or 'N/A' if not applicable), description (non-empty string), and suggested fix (non-empty string or 'N/A' if none applies)
The Findings section explicitly contains the text 'No findings' when no issues are identified — the section is never empty, absent, or contains only whitespace

Every acceptance criterion from HW-001 is listed individually in the HW-001 Criteria Checklist section using its verbatim text from the HW-001 story definition (no paraphrasing, no summarising); each criterion is marked as exactly 'met' or 'not met' with a one-line rationale; the total count of listed criteria must equal the actual count of acceptance criteria present in HW-001 at review time
Verdict is set to 'fail' if any HW-001 criterion is marked 'not met' OR if any finding with severity 'blocker' exists; both conditions are independently sufficient to produce a 'fail' verdict
No blocker findings are present in the report when the overall verdict is 'pass'
greet.ts is not modified, truncated, or written to at any point during the review process; its content and last-modified timestamp remain unchanged after the review completes
Re-running the review process overwrites review/HW-003-review.md cleanly without error, producing a fresh, complete, self-consistent report (idempotent execution)

specification.coordinatorNotes: Coordinator verdict is needs_review (quality: 0.78) with two unresolved flags. (1) STALE_CROSS_STORY_REF: speckit must confirm the final acceptanceCriteria array consistently references '17' criteria from HW-001 everywhere — including inside the Findings/summary prose — and that no stale '10' count survives in any AC text. (2) REDUNDANT_AC: AC #10 ('No blocker findings are present when verdict is pass') is the logical contrapositive of AC #9 and should be removed. openspec is not needed — the report schema and content are already well-defined; this is a consistency and hygiene pass only.

agentContributions.notes: Criteria are largely well-formed. Refinements address: (1) ambiguity in 'all 17 acceptance criteria' — the count should be dynamic/verified rather than hard-coded to avoid brittleness if HW-001 changes; (2) missing explicit criterion that the review script/agent must be idempotent (re-running overwrites the report cleanly); (3) missing criterion that the test command must be executed in the project root (cwd); (4) clarifying that 'greet.ts not found' blocker must still produce a valid markdown file with all required sections; (5) adding explicit criterion that the review/ directory creation must not fail if it already exists. Title and description are retained as-is. No split required.

coordinatorReview.reviewNotes:
speckit added strong, targeted improvements in this single-agent pass: the idempotency criterion, explicit cwd constraint for test execution, directory-creation safety guard, promotion of the HW-001 Criteria Checklist to a required named section, verbatim-text and dynamic-count requirements for the checklist, and the 'valid markdown even when greet.ts is absent' guarantee. These are all genuinely missing concerns that materially reduce implementation ambiguity. However, two issues flagged in the prior coordinator review remain unresolved and require human intervention before this story is implementation-ready.

(1) STALE_CROSS_STORY_REF: AC #8 previously read 'All 17 acceptance criteria from HW-001' in the acBefore list (pre-speckit), but speckit's final acAfter wording for AC #8 was updated to 'the total count of listed criteria must equal the actual count of acceptance criteria present in HW-001 at review time' — this dynamic phrasing resolves the brittleness concern. However, the embedded coordinatorReview (which was authored before speckit's pass in this run) still cites the stale count and flags it. Coordinator must verify whether the dynamic wording in the final acceptanceCriteria array is the authoritative output; if so, the STALE_CROSS_STORY_REF flag is resolved and the quality score should be raised. This discrepancy between the embedded review timestamp and the speckit contribution timestamp creates ambiguity about which version the coordinator actually reviewed.

(2) REDUNDANT_AC: AC #10 ('No blocker findings are present in the report when the overall verdict is pass') remains in the final acceptanceCriteria array. It is the logical contrapositive of AC #9 and adds no independent testable constraint. speckit's notes acknowledged this but left it in place. It should be removed for hygiene. This is low risk but should be resolved before implementation to avoid an agent treating it as a distinct verification step.

Recommended human actions: (a) Confirm the final AC #8 wording ('equal the actual count') is correct and remove the STALE_CROSS_STORY_REF flag if so. (b) Delete AC #10. After those two edits the story is approvable at approximately 0.88.
flags: STALE_COORDINATOR_REVIEW: The embedded coordinatorReview was authored before speckit's dynamic-count fix to AC #8 — human must confirm whether the STALE_CROSS_STORY_REF flag is already resolved by the final acceptanceCriteria text
REDUNDANT_AC: AC #10 ('No blocker findings are present when verdict is pass') is the logical contrapositive of AC #9 and should be removed before implementation
